title: Business Credit Card
description: One hand-curated BUSINESS credit card (plan 22). Rates are in earn-units per dollar, valued via data/business/meta/point-valuations.yaml. Forked from the consumer card schema: the FICO approval block is replaced by business_approval (entity/guarantee/cash/revenue underwriting), fees by pricing (annual-fee vs per-seat SaaS models), and business-specific mechanics are added (employee_cards, adaptive_top_n, min_transaction_usd, payment_type, pooling, integrations). Registry membership is enforced by scripts/validate_business_cards.py, not by this schema.

id.description: Stable slug, must match the filename (without .yaml)

issuer.description: Issuer slug, must match the parent directory name and exist in data/business/meta/issuer-rules.yaml

availability.description: Same semantics as the consumer schema: discontinued cards are excluded from Auto recommendations but stay scorable in manual mode

program.description: Key into data/business/meta/point-valuations.yaml (use 'cash' for cash-back cards)

base_rate.description: Catch-all earn rate applied to spend not matched by any category/merchant reward or adaptive_top_n line

base_rate_cap.description: Cap on the BASE rate itself, for flat-rate-capped business cards (Amex Blue Business Plus/Cash: 2x/2% on the first $50,000 of ALL purchases each calendar year, then 1x/1%). base_rate is the elevated headline rate; above max_spend_usd the card earns fallback_rate. Never model this as identical category lines across the whole enum

portal.description: Issuer travel-portal slug (amex_travel, chase_travel, capital_one_travel) named in portal-rate notes. Required by the validator when any category reward or credit is portal_only. Free-form slug — the business dataset has no statement-descriptors registry (no statement parsing, plan 22)

unlocks_transfers.description: true = holding this card unlocks its program's transfer partners for every same-program card in the scored portfolio (Ink Business Preferred for UR). Only on programs marked transfer_gateway_required in point-valuations.yaml (validator-enforced). The FULL personal/business interaction model (plan 22) also lets a PERSONAL gateway card from the profile (personal.premium_cards_held) unlock these programs — that mapping lives in the optimizer, not card data

max_annual_rewards_usd.description: Card-wide cap on total reward value earnable per year, in USD. Omit when uncapped

adaptive_top_n.description: Amex Business Gold mechanic: the card's top n eligible categories by the cardholder's own spend earn `rate`, everything else falls to base_rate. Deterministic given a spend profile — the optimizer picks the user's n highest-spend eligible categories and materializes them as capped category lines. At most one per card; a card with adaptive_top_n may not also list any of its eligible_categories in category_rewards (validator-enforced)

n.description: How many top categories earn the elevated rate (Amex Business Gold: 2)

eligible_categories.description: The fixed menu the top-n selection draws from; must have more entries than n

note.description: Issuer's own wording of the selection mechanic (per-cycle vs calendar-year selection, etc.)

large_purchase_rate.description: Category-AGNOSTIC elevated rate on individual purchases of at least min_transaction_usd, in any category (Amex Business Platinum 2x on $5k+ purchases; Ink Premier 2.5% on $5k+). Distinct from a category line's min_transaction_usd, which stays within its category. The optimizer earns this rate on each bucket's spend × the profile's large_txn_share when it beats the bucket's best category line; cap may share a pool with category lines via shared_cap_id (Amex $2M pool spans both). Rate must exceed base_rate (validator-enforced)

kind.description: Same semantics as the consumer schema: in_kind = curator-estimated non-cash benefit, always haircut

amount_points.description: Points granted per period instead of USD. Points cards only

category.description: Optional business category this credit offsets (key from categories.yaml)

usage_keys.description: Usage-question item keys (data/business/meta/usage-questions.yaml), anyOf semantics — the credit is $0 unless the user confirmed at least one (Dell credit is worthless to a company that doesn't buy Dell)

automatic.description: true = pays with no merchant-specific action (anniversary-style). Mutually exclusive with usage_keys and category; every credit must carry at least one of the three (validator-enforced, same as consumer)

portal_only.description: Redeemable only through the issuer's travel portal — requires the card-level portal key

signup_bonus.description: null when the card has no current public signup bonus. Business SUBs are large; spend feasibility is checked against company spend routed to the card, honoring employee_cards.spend_counts_toward_bonus

tiers.description: Additional tranches at higher CUMULATIVE spend in the same window; requirements must exceed the base and strictly ascend (validator-enforced)

pricing.description: Cost model. annual_fee = traditional per-card annual fee. per_seat = fintech SaaS pricing (Ramp/Brex/Divvy): the CARD costs nothing at the free tier — the optimizer scores $0 with the paid tier disclosed via per_seat_monthly_usd + platform_fee_note (paid tiers buy software, not card economics; plan 21 §6)

fee_refund_spend_usd.description: Annual card spend at which the issuer refunds the annual fee (Spark Cash Plus: $150 back at $150k). The optimizer refunds the fee when spend routed to the card reaches this

per_seat_monthly_usd.description: Paid-tier price per user per month (disclosure only when a $0 free tier exists)

free_tier.description: per_seat cards: true when a $0 tier exists covering card issuance + basic controls (Ramp, Brex Essentials). Scored at $0; required for per_seat in V1 (the optimizer has no paid-tier scoring yet — validator-enforced)

platform_fee_note.description: What the paid tier adds and any platform fee (Ramp Plus $15/user/mo + platform fee)

business_approval.description: Replaces the consumer FICO approval block (plan 21 §4). Business eligibility = entity type + guarantee model + business-financial thresholds. Issuers don't publish cutoffs — curator estimates, reasoning in notes.

personal_guarantee.description: true = owner personally guarantees the debt (SSN application, personal credit pulled) — tier 1/2 issuer cards. false = EIN-anchored corporate liability (Ramp/Brex/Divvy)

min_personal_fico_tier.description: Owner's personal credit needed for likely approval. Only meaningful (and only allowed) when personal_guarantee is true (validator-enforced)

entity_types.description: Entity types the issuer accepts. Sole props qualify for most tier-1 cards with SSN only; fintech corporate cards typically require llc/corp + EIN

min_cash_balance_usd.description: Minimum linked business-bank balance for approval (Ramp $25k, Divvy $20k, Brex ~$50k funded-startup path)

min_annual_revenue_usd.description: Minimum annual revenue path, when the issuer publishes one (Brex $500k). When both cash and revenue paths exist, satisfying EITHER qualifies (validator allows both present)

funding_qualifies.description: true when institutional equity funding satisfies underwriting in place of cash/revenue thresholds (Brex)

notes.description: Issuer-specific approval rules worth knowing (5/24 applies to applications, once-per-lifetime bonus, US-incorporation requirements)

employee_cards.description: Employee-card economics (plan 21 §3). Employee spend pools to the central account at normal rates, so it is just company spend; what varies per card is the per-seat fee and controls. The optimizer charges employee_card_seats × fee_usd as an ongoing cost (0 when free).

fee_usd.description: Annual fee per employee card ($0 Chase/Capital One, $95 Amex Business Gold, $400 Employee Business Platinum)

free_expense_card_variant.description: true when a $0 employee-card variant exists alongside a paid one (Amex Employee Business Expense Cards). V1 optimizer still charges fee_usd — the free variant is a disclosure, since its benefits differ; note the tradeoff in notes

max_cards.description: Issuer cap on employee cards per account (Amex 99). Omit when unpublished

spend_counts_toward_bonus.description: false when employee-card spend is excluded from signup-bonus spend requirements (US Bank Triple Cash). V1: recorded and surfaced as a note; feasibility still uses total spend (no owner/employee split in the profile)

controls.description: Spend-control features, unscored — surfaced as UI badges. preswipe_policy = policy enforced before the transaction completes (Ramp/Brex)

payment_type.description: revolving = balance can be carried at interest (most tier-1 issuer cards). charge = pay in full each cycle (Amex charge lineage, Spark Cash Plus, all fintech corporate cards). Charge cards are exempt from the Amex 5-credit-card limit (issuer-rules.yaml charge_exempt)

pooling.description: Only for cards that BREAK their program's normal pooling (Ink Business Premier: UR-denominated but cannot combine with other Chase cards or transfer to partners). Omit for normal cards — omission means the program's standard pooling applies.

integrations.description: Native accounting/ERP integrations (plan 21 §6) — unscored in V1, surfaced as badges and filterable in the UI. partner_integrations = connects only via third-party connectors (Amex)

virtual_cards.description: true = issuer supports virtual card numbers on this product (fintech tier: unlimited). Unscored, UI badge

float_days.description: Working-capital float (plan 21 §6): days between purchase and payment due beyond the cycle close. Reported per portfolio (average weighted by assigned spend), never scored in V1.

grace_days.description: Grace period after statement close (typically 21-25 for revolving, issuer-defined for charge)

benefit_flags.description: Binary attributes (lounge_access, cell_phone_protection, primary_rental_insurance) — not scored in V1

sources.description: Same provenance contract as the consumer schema: exact links pasted at the moment a fact enters the file, each declaring which blocks it supports.

confidence.description: high = checked against issuer terms; medium = reputable secondary source; low = drafted, needs human verification

fallback_rate.description: Earn rate on spend above the cap

shared_cap_id.description: Combined-pool marker, same as the consumer schema — business caps are USUALLY combined across categories (Ink Cash: 5% office supply + telecom on ONE $25k pool; Ink Preferred: 3x across four categories on ONE $150k pool). Every entry with the same id states the identical full pool (period + max_spend_usd); the optimizer drains one pool across them

bonusValue.description: At least one of points / usd; both for mixed bonuses

min_transaction_usd.description: The rate applies only to INDIVIDUAL purchases of at least this size (Amex Business Platinum 2x on $5k+ purchases; Ink Premier 2.5% on $5k+). The optimizer earns this rate on eligible-bucket spend × the profile's large_txn_share; the remainder falls through to the next line

portal_only.description: true if the rate only applies when booked through the issuer's portal (requires the card-level portal key)
